classes.reasoning: This document is an internal corporate email thread discussing the preparation and finalization of a 2022 Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Report for Biorce. It contains environmental performance metrics, employee statistics, and governance policies but is not a clinical trial artifact, regulatory submission, patient-facing document, or any recognized TMF component. It is corporate communications material unrelated to clinical trial execution.

summary: This is an email thread export from Biorce employees discussing the draft and final versions of the company's 2022 ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) Report. The emails cover internal review comments on emissions reductions, employee wellbeing policies, and corporate sustainability commitments, intended for Board review on 24 March 2023. It is not a clinical trial document.
content:
================================================================================
EMAIL THREAD EXPORT — BIORCE INTERNAL
Thread: Draft ESG Report 2022 -- ready for your review
================================================================================

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FROM:    Lucia Mendoza <l.mendoza@biorce.com>
TO:      Rafael Moreno <r.moreno@biorce.com>
CC:      Carmen Lopez <c.lopez@biorce.com>; Javier Ortega <j.ortega@biorce.com>
DATE:    14 March 2023, 16:42
SUBJECT: Draft ESG Report 2022 -- ready for your review
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Rafael,

Please find attached the draft ESG Report 2022 for review ahead of the
Board meeting on 24 March.

Key highlights:

ENVIRONMENT
- Scope 1+2 emissions: 184 tCO2e (down 12% from 2021 -- renewable electricity
  switch April 2022 + reduced travel)
- Business travel down 23% -- virtual investigator meetings helped significantly
- Lab waste: 98.4% via accredited hazardous waste channels (target: 100%)
- Printing down 67% since eTMF rollout in Q2 2022 (~840 kg paper saved annually)

SOCIAL
- 47 FTE as of December 2022 (+ 3 part-time contractors)
- Gender: 57% female overall; 50% female in senior leadership
- Voluntary attrition: 8.5% (vs 13% industry benchmark -- positive)
- New: flexible working policy (3 remote days/week)
- Enhanced parental leave (20 weeks primary / 8 weeks secondary carer)
- EUR 500/employee annual wellbeing budget

GOVERNANCE
- Code of Ethics and Anti-Bribery Policy approved January 2022
- 100% employee training completion by March 2022
- Zero violations reported in 2022

I've highlighted Pages 4 and 8 for your sign-off before we finalise.

Can you return by COB Friday 17 March?

Lucia

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FROM:    Rafael Moreno <r.moreno@biorce.com>
TO:      Lucia Mendoza <l.mendoza@biorce.com>
DATE:    16 March 2023, 09:28
SUBJECT: RE: Draft ESG Report 2022 -- ready for your review
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Lucia,

Great work. Two comments:

Page 4 (climate targets): Add a forward-looking commitment -- net-zero
Scope 1+2 by 2030, and Scope 3 baseline assessment in 2023. Check with
Javier that this is operationally realistic before finalising.

Page 8 (office energy rating): The building EPC is a "D" -- let's not
highlight it. Instead add a note that we are exploring green lease addendum
options with the landlord (Inversiones Castellana 41) as part of 2023
sustainability roadmap. This is factually accurate.

The people & culture section is particularly strong.

R.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FROM:    Lucia Mendoza <l.mendoza@biorce.com>
TO:      Biorce-Board <board@biorce.com>
DATE:    17 March 2023, 17:55
SUBJECT: Biorce ESG Report 2022 -- FINAL for Board Review
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Dear Board Members,

Please find attached the final ESG Report 2022 for review at the Board
meeting on 24 March 2023.

Changes incorporated per Rafael's comments:
  - Page 4: 2030 Scope 1+2 net-zero target; Scope 3 baseline in 2023
  - Page 8: EPC rating removed; green lease addendum language added

Lucia
================================================================================
